en: If your company performs any of the following services and is required to maintain the relevant certification(s) for those services, you will be asked to provide your certificate in a subsequent form.
Canada Commercial Vehicle Operator's Registration Certificate (CVOR) Applicability Assessment

If your vehicle is plated in Ontario, the United States or Mexico and operating in Ontario, you need a CVOR for:
  • trucks with a registered gross weight (RGW) or actual weight over 4,500 kg, including trucks with farm license plates
  • buses with a seating capacity of 10 or more passengers
  • concrete pumps and mobile cranes (not including off-road mobile cranes)
  • tow trucks, including:
    • vehicles with a flatbed that can tilt to load and are used exclusively to tow or transport other motor vehicles
    • any motor vehicle designed, modified, configured or equipped to tow another motor vehicle
Does your company operate commercial vehicles in Ontario, Canada and required to maintain a corporation-level Commercial Vehicle Operator's Registration Certificate (CVOR)?
Please select from the following reasons your company is exempt from needing a corporation-level Commercial Vehicle Operator's Registration Certificate (CVOR).
The company's trucks or buses are  plated in another Canadian province or territory.
The company's trucks (other than a tow truck) are with registered gross weight or gross weight of 4,500 kg or less.
The company's vehicles have a manufacturer's gross vehicle weight rating not exceeding 6,500 kg (14,330 lb.).
The company's vehicles are fitted with either the original, unmodified box installed by the manufacturer, or an unmodified replacement box that duplicates the one installed by the manufacturer.
The company's vehicles are not carrying or towing a trailer carrying commercial cargo or tools, or equipment of any type normally used for commercial purposes.
Please explain why your company is exempt from needing a corporation-level Commercial Vehicle Operator's Registration Certificate (CVOR).
